signals.title: Tech Aspiration vs Demonstrated Technical Practice
signals.evidence_anchor: Essay emphasizes solving real-world problems through tech innovation (ESS-001:F01/F02) while activity profile shows Olympiads participation but no self-directed technical projects.
signals.direct_read: Strong conceptual interest in technology's societal impact is articulated, but activity profile emphasizes academic competitions and music rather than hands-on engineering/CS work.
signals.depth_opening: Whether this applicant's technical orientation is primarily theoretical or has been tested/developed through applied problem-solving outside classroom contexts.
signals.why_it_matters: Clarifies how their stated passion for 'designing solutions' connects to lived experience beyond academic achievement.

signals.title: Empathy-Driven Leadership Identity
signals.evidence_anchor: Community dog welfare project (ESS-002) frames leadership through listening and persuasion, contrasting with formal House Captain role.
signals.direct_read: Leadership essay emphasizes grassroots community engagement and emotional intelligence over positional authority.
signals.depth_opening: How this applicant reconciles two distinct leadership models - institutional (House Captain) and community-organizing (stray animal project) - in their self-concept.
signals.why_it_matters: Reveals whether their leadership philosophy is consciously integrated or contextually adaptive.

signals.title: Interdisciplinary Claims Without Visible Cross-Training
signals.evidence_anchor: Essay states interest in blending tech with design/data/human behavior (ESS-001:F04), but activities show deep specialization in STEM/music.
signals.direct_read: Claims interdisciplinary orientation while demonstrating sustained commitment to domain-specific pursuits (7 years Olympiads, 5 years piano).
signals.depth_opening: How concrete experiences of synthesis across domains have shaped their understanding of interdisciplinary work versus aspirational interest.
signals.why_it_matters: Distinguishes between conceptual appreciation of interdisciplinarity and demonstrated capacity to integrate disparate fields.

signals.title: Academic Transition Without Narrative
signals.evidence_anchor: Board change from CBSE to Telangana State between 10th-11th grades with maintained high performance (ACA-002/ACA-003).
signals.direct_read: Successfully navigated significant curriculum shift without apparent academic disruption.
signals.depth_opening: How this transition influenced their learning strategies or self-concept beyond numerical outcomes.
signals.why_it_matters: Reveals adaptability skills and meta-cognitive awareness not captured by grades alone.

themes.title: Integration of Conceptual and Applied Tech Identity
themes.unifying_axis: The relationship between this applicant's strongly articulated technology philosophy and their demonstrated modes of engagement with technical challenges.
themes.interview_direction: Explore how classroom achievements, extracurricular activities, and personal projects have collectively shaped their understanding of technology's role in problem-solving.

themes.title: Leadership as Contextual Adaptation
themes.unifying_axis: The applicant's ability to flex leadership approaches between formal institutional roles and grassroots community initiatives.
themes.interview_direction: Understand how different leadership experiences inform their approach to motivating others and driving change in varied environments.

themes.title: Resilience Through Educational Transitions
themes.unifying_axis: The interplay between academic adaptability and personal growth during structural changes in learning environment.
themes.interview_direction: Surface the strategies and self-awareness developed through navigating curriculum shifts while maintaining excellence.
